primary_link.title: mistralai/Mistral-Medium-3.5-128B

mistral-ai.summary: Mistral Medium 3.5 is our first flagship merged model. It is a dense 128B model with a 256k context window, handling instruction-following, reasoning, and coding in a single set of weights. Mistral Medium 3.5 replaces its predecessor Mistral Medium 3.1 and Magistral in Le Chat. It also replaces Devstral 2 in our coding agent Vibe. Concretely, expect better performance for instruct, reasoning and coding tasks in a new unified model in comparison with our previous released models. Reasoning effort is configurable per request, so the same model can answer a quick chat reply or work through a complex agentic run. We trained the vision encoder from scratch to handle variable image sizes and aspect ratios. Find more information on our blog. > To speed up local inference using vLLM or SGLang,...

qwen.summary: We are excited to introduce Qwen-Scope, an interpretability module trained on the Qwen3 and Qwen3.5 series models. Specifically, we integrated and trained Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) within Qwen’s hidden layers. By implementing sparsity constraints, we can automatically extract data features that are highly decoupled, low-redundancy, and significantly more interpretable. Qwen-Scope can be used not only to analyze the internal mechanisms of Qwen’s behavior but also holds immense potential for model optimization. Application scenarios include steerable inference control, evaluation sample distribution analysis and comparison, data classification and synthesis, and model training and optimization. links.title: Latent Adversarial Detection: Adaptive Probing of LLM Activations for Multi-Turn Attack Detection

primary_link.title: Latent Adversarial Detection: Adaptive Probing of LLM Activations for Multi-Turn Attack Detection

papers.summary: Multi-turn prompt injection follows a known attack path -- trust-building, pivoting, escalation but text-level defenses miss covert attacks where individual turns appear benign. We show this attack path leaves an activation-level signature in the model's residual stream: each phase shift moves the activation, producing a total path length far exceeding benign conversations. We call this adversarial restlessness. Five scalar trajectory features capturing this signal lift conversation-level detect
papers.summary_zh: 多輪 prompt injection 遵循已知的攻擊路徑——建立信任、轉向、升級，但文本級防禦措施會錯過單獨輪次看起來無害的隱蔽攻擊。我們發現這種攻擊路徑在模型的 residual stream 中留下激活級別的特徵：每個相移都會移動激活，產生遠超良性對話的總路徑長度。我們稱之為 adversarial restlessness。五個捕捉此信號的標量軌跡特徵提升了對話級別的偵測能力。
papers.title: Latent Adversarial Detection: Adaptive Probing of LLM Activations for Multi-Turn Attack Detection
papers.title_zh: 潛在對抗性偵測：對 LLM 激活進行自適應探測以實現多輪攻擊偵測

links.title: TwinGate: Stateful Defense against Decompositional Jailbreaks in Untraceable Traffic via Asymmetric Contrastive Learning

primary_link.title: TwinGate: Stateful Defense against Decompositional Jailbreaks in Untraceable Traffic via Asymmetric Contrastive Learning

papers.summary: Decompositional jailbreaks pose a critical threat to large language models (LLMs) by allowing adversaries to fragment a malicious objective into a sequence of individually benign queries that collectively reconstruct prohibited content. In real-world deployments, LLMs face a continuous, untraceable stream of fully anonymized and arbitrarily interleaved requests, infiltrated by covertly distributed adversarial queries. Under this rigorous threat model, state-of-the-art defensive strategies exhibi
papers.summary_zh: Decompositional jailbreaks 對大型語言模型 (LLMs) 構成嚴重威脅，因為它們允許攻擊者將惡意目標分解成一系列單獨無害的查詢，這些查詢共同重構被禁止的內容。在實際部署中，LLMs 面臨連續、不可追溯的、完全匿名且任意交錯的請求流，其中滲透著秘密分發的對抗性查詢。在這種嚴格的威脅模型下，最先進的防禦策略展現出其局限性。
papers.title: TwinGate: Stateful Defense against Decompositional Jailbreaks in Untraceable Traffic via Asymmetric Contrastive Learning
papers.title_zh: TwinGate：透過非對稱對比學習對抗不可追溯流量中分解式越獄的有狀態防禦

links.title: Geometry-Calibrated Conformal Abstention for Language Models

primary_link.title: Geometry-Calibrated Conformal Abstention for Language Models

papers.summary: When language models lack relevant knowledge for a given query, they frequently generate plausible responses that can be hallucinations, rather than admitting being agnostic about the answer. Retraining models to reward admitting ignorance can lead to overly conservative behaviors and poor generalization due to scarce evaluation benchmarks. We propose a post hoc framework, Conformal Abstention (CA), adapted from conformal prediction (CP) to determine whether to abstain from answering a query. CA
papers.summary_zh: 當語言模型缺乏針對給定查詢的相關知識時，它們經常生成看似合理的回答，這些回答可能是 hallucination，而不是承認對答案一無所知。重新訓練模型以獎勵承認無知可能會導致過於保守的行為，並由於評估基準稀缺而導致泛化能力差。我們提出了一個事後框架，Conformal Abstention (CA)，它改編自 conformal prediction (CP)，以決定是否拒絕回答查詢。CA...

links.title: RHyVE: Competence-Aware Verification and Phase-Aware Deployment for LLM-Generated Reward Hypotheses

primary_link.title: RHyVE: Competence-Aware Verification and Phase-Aware Deployment for LLM-Generated Reward Hypotheses

papers.summary: Large language models (LLMs) make reward design in reinforcement learning substantially more scalable, but generated rewards are not automatically reliable training objectives. Existing work has focused primarily on generating, evolving, or selecting reward candidates, while paying less attention to when such candidates can be verified and deployed during policy optimization. We study this deployment-time problem by treating generated rewards as reward hypotheses whose utility depends on the com
papers.summary_zh: 大型語言模型 (LLMs) 使 reinforcement learning 中的獎勵設計顯著更具擴展性，但生成的獎勵並非自動可靠的訓練目標。現有工作主要集中在生成、演化或選擇獎勵候選者，而較少關注這些候選者何時可以在 policy optimization 期間被驗證和部署。我們透過將生成的獎勵視為獎勵假設來研究這個部署時問題，這些假設的實用性取決於其完整性。

links.title: Characterizing the Consistency of the Emergent Misalignment Persona

primary_link.title: Characterizing the Consistency of the Emergent Misalignment Persona

papers.summary: Fine-tuning large language models (LLMs) on narrowly misaligned data generalizes to broadly misaligned behavior, a phenomenon termed emergent misalignment (EM). While prior work has found a correlation between harmful behavior and self-assessment in emergently misaligned models, it remains unclear how consistent this correspondence is across tasks and whether it varies across fine-tuning domains. We characterize the consistency of the EM persona by fine-tuning Qwen 2.5 32B Instruct on six narrow
papers.summary_zh: 在狹隘錯位資料上對 large language models (LLMs) 進行 fine-tuning 會泛化為廣泛錯位行為，這種現象被稱為 emergent misalignment (EM)。儘管先前的研究發現 emergently misaligned models 中的有害行為與自我評估之間存在相關性，但這種對應關係在不同任務間的一致性以及其是否隨 fine-tuning 領域而異仍不明確。我們透過在六個狹隘領域上 fine-tuning Qwen 2.5 32B Instruct 來表徵 EM persona 的一致性

links.title: Kernelized Advantage Estimation: From Nonparametric Statistics to LLM Reasoning

primary_link.title: Kernelized Advantage Estimation: From Nonparametric Statistics to LLM Reasoning

papers.summary: Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) have increasingly relied on reinforcement learning (RL) to improve their reasoning capabilities. radar.summary: Surprisal theory links human processing effort to the predictability of an upcoming linguistic unit, but empirical work often leaves the notion of a unit underspecified. In practice, experimental stimuli are segmented into linguistically motivated units (e.g., words), while pretrained language models assign probability mass to a fixed token alphabet that typically does not align with those units. radar.summary_zh: Surprisal theory 將人類處理努力（processing effort）與即將出現的語言單位（linguistic unit）的可預測性聯繫起來，但實證研究通常對「單位」的概念定義不清。在實踐中，實驗刺激被分割成具有語言學動機的單位（例如 words），而 pretrained language models 則將概率質量（probability mass）分配給一個固定的 token alphabet，該 alphabet 通常與這些單位不對齊。因此，基於 surprisal 的預測器隱含地依賴於將兩種不同事物混淆的 ad hoc 程序。

radar.title: SpatialGrammar: A Domain-Specific Language for LLM-Based 3D Indoor Scene Generation

radar.summary_zh: 大型語言模型 (LLM) 越來越多地用於 recommendation reranking，但其 listwise 預測可能取決於候選項目呈現的順序。這在推薦的 set-based 特性與 decoder-only LLM 的 sequence-based 計算之間產生了不匹配，其中重新排列一個原本相同的候選集可能會改變項目分數和最終排名。這種 order sensitivity 使得基於 LLM 的 rerankers 難以依賴，因為排名可能反映 prompt
radar.title: One Pass, Any Order: Position-Invariant Listwise Reranking for LLM-Based Recommendation

links.title: Visual Generation in the New Era: An Evolution from Atomic Mapping to Agentic World Modeling

primary_link.title: Visual Generation in the New Era: An Evolution from Atomic Mapping to Agentic World Modeling

radar.summary: Recent visual generation models have made major progress in photorealism, typography, instruction following, and interactive editing, yet they still struggle with spatial reasoning, persistent state, long-horizon consistency, and causal understanding. radar.summary_zh: 大型語言模型 (LLMs) 越來越多地被用作城市分析中人類感知的代理，然而，persona prompting 是否能產生有意義且可重現的行為多樣性仍不明確。我們研究了不同的 persona 是否會影響多模態 LLMs 生成的城市情感判斷。我們使用了一組包含性別、經濟狀況、政治傾向和個性的 factorial personas，為每個 persona 實例化了多個 agent，以評估城市場景圖像 f

primary_link.title: Can AI Be a Good Peer Reviewer? A Survey of Peer Review Process, Evaluation, and the Future

radar.summary: Peer review is a multi-stage process involving reviews, rebuttals, meta-reviews, final decisions, and subsequent manuscript revisions. links.title: Sun Finance automates ID extraction and fraud detection with generative AI on AWS

primary_link.title: Sun Finance automates ID extraction and fraud detection with generative AI on AWS

radar.summary: In this post, we show how Sun Finance used Amazon Bedrock, Amazon Textract, and Amazon Rekognition to build an AI-powered identity verification (IDV) pipeline. The solution improved extraction accuracy from 79.7% to 90.8%, cut per-document costs by 91%, and reduced processing time from up to 20 hours to under 5 seconds. You'll learn how combining specialized OCR with large language model (LLM) structuring outperformed using either tool alone. You'll also learn how to architect a serverless fraud detection system using vector similarity search.
radar.title: Sun Finance automates ID extraction and fraud detection with generative AI on AWS

radar.summary: In this post, you will configure Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Gateway to access private endpoints using Resource Gateway, a managed construct that provisions Elastic Network Interfaces (ENIs) directly inside your Amazon VPC, one per subnet. You will explore two implementation modes (managed and self-managed) and walk through three practical scenarios: connecting to a private Amazon API Gateway endpoint, integrating with a MCP server on Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS), and accessing a private REST API.
